name: Ubuntu 22.04 LTS
notes: Jammy Jellyfish. Most CI/CD pipelines target this release. Supported until April 2027. Very broad community support.

name: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS
notes: Noble Numbat. Ships with Python 3.12. Default WSL install as of 2024. Supported until April 2029.

name: Debian
notes: Debian 12 Bookworm. Smaller footprint than Ubuntu. Very stable; package versions lag behind Ubuntu.

name: Arch Linux (unofficial)
install-cmd:
# Download from https://github.com/yuk7/ArchWSL/releases then:
wsl --import Arch C:\WSL\Arch arch.tar.zst
notes: Rolling release. Not in Microsoft Store — install via the ArchWSL community project. Full AUR access via yay/paru.

name: Kali Linux
notes: Security-focused rolling Debian derivative with pentesting tools. GUI via kali-win-kex. Default install is minimal; add tools with kali-linux-default.

name: openSUSE Tumbleweed
notes: Rolling release openSUSE. Uses RPM packages and zypper. Available on Microsoft Store.

name: Fedora Remix for WSL
notes: Community-maintained Fedora 39 for WSL. Available on Microsoft Store and via winget. Uses DNF5 package manager.

name: Alpine Linux
notes: Extremely minimal musl-based distro (~20 MB). No systemd. Ideal for quick scripts and container-like workflows.

name: Oracle Linux 8
notes: RHEL-compatible Oracle Linux 8.7. Good for testing RHEL-targeted applications. Free to use.

name: SUSE Linux Enterprise (SLES)
notes: SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP5. Enterprise-grade stability. Available on Microsoft Store. Requires SUSE subscription for updates.
